fr:title.text: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:meta.name: venue

fr:link.title: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar 37

fr:meta.fr:link: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar 37

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The idea of synthetic domain theory is to work in the internal language of a topos containing an interval object that forms a dominance and satisfies a few other axioms, such as Phoa’s principle. Then, not all objects deserve to be called “predomains”, but the ones that do invariably arise within full internal reflective subcategories defined by simple orthogonality laws stated in terms of the interval’s geometry. It so happens that some of these orthogonality laws are also of use in defining synthetic ∞-categories à la Riehl and Shulman.

I will outline some recent results in synthetic (higher) domain theory obtained with my former Masters student Leoni Pugh concerning partial map classifiers, including (1) the closure of synthetic ∞-categories under partial map classifiers, and (2) the discovery of a strengthening of the Segal completeness law that, amongst synthetic partial orders, causes the partial map classifier to coincide with the Sierpiński cone.
(See our joint paper When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?, to appear in LICS ’25: 40th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science.)

fr:title.text: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: When is the partial map classifier a Sierpiński cone?

fr:meta.name: venue

fr:link.title: LICS ’25: 40th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science

fr:meta.fr:link: LICS ’25: 40th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science

fr:mainmatter.html:p: We study the relationship between partial map classifiers, Sierpiński cones, and axioms for synthetic higher categories and domains within univalent foundations. In particular, we show that synthetic ∞-categories are closed under partial map classifiers assuming Phoa's principle, and we isolate a new reflective subuniverse of types within which the Sierpiński cone (a lax colimit) can be computed as a partial map classifier by strengthening the Segal condition.

The paper currently states that the alternative type theoretic computation of the scone uses the flattening lemma, but this is incorrect. In fact, we need only the fact the dependent sum functor preserves colimits (being left adjoint to base change). This will be corrected in the final version of the paper.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: A type theory for synthetic ∞-categories
fr:frontmatter.fr:meta: @article{riehl-shulman-2017, author = {Riehl, Emily and Shulman, Michael}, url = {https://journals.mq.edu.au/index.php/higher_structures/article/view/36}, year = {2017}, eprint = {1705.07442}, eprintclass = {math.CT}, eprinttype = {arXiv}, issue = {1}, journal = {Higher Structures}, pages = {147--224}, title = {A type theory for synthetic $\infty$-categories}, volume = {1}, }

fr:mainmatter.html:p: We propose foundations for a synthetic theory of (∞,1)-categories within homotopy type theory. We axiomatize a directed interval type, then define higher simplices from it and use them to probe the internal categorical structures of arbitrary types. We define Segal types, in which binary composites exist uniquely up to homotopy; this automatically ensures composition is coherently associative and unital at all dimensions. We define Rezk types, in which the categorical isomorphisms are additionally equivalent to the type-theoretic identities - a “local univalence” condition. And we define covariant fibrations, which are type families varying functorially over a Segal type, and prove a “dependent Yoneda lemma” that can be viewed as a directed form of the usual elimination rule for identity types. We conclude by studying homotopically correct adjunctions between Segal types, and showing that for a functor between Rezk types to have an adjoint is a mere proposition.

To make the bookkeeping in such proofs manageable, we use a three-layered type theory with shapes, whose contexts are extended by polytopes within directed cubes, which can be abstracted over using “extension types” that generalize the path-types of cubical type theory. In an appendix, we describe the motivating semantics in the Reedy model structure on bisimplicial sets, in which our Segal and Rezk types correspond to Segal spaces and complete Segal spaces.

fr:title.text: LICS ’25: 40th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: LICS ’25: 40th Annual ACM/IEEE Symposium on Logic in Computer Science

fr:mainmatter.html:p: The LICS Symposium is an annual international forum on theoretical and practical topics in computer science that relate to logic, broadly construed.

fr:title.text: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar 37

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Yorkshire and Midlands Category Theory Seminar 37
